preview_text:
The model gives USA a 44% chance to win, making them the statistical favorite, while Paraguay enters with a 25% win probability. The gap is meaningful but not dominant—Paraguay retains a genuine path to victory, particularly through either team's failure to convert chances or defensive lapses rather than a heavy underdog upset.

The 31% draw probability shapes this as a competitive encounter with a wide feasible range of outcomes. The spread across all three results is relatively even, suggesting neither outcome would constitute a major surprise. A one-goal margin in either direction or a goalless draw are all plausible terminal states given the model's distribution.

The sportsbook consensus aligns closely with the model across all three outcomes, with gaps of 4–5 percentage points. Both sources treat USA as slight favorites while crediting Paraguay meaningful winning chances. Polymarket prices, by contrast, assign nearly equal weight to all three outcomes (each around 33%), a substantially flatter view than either the model or the books. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better reflects ground truth.
